"The biggest and best woman in the world," an 82-foot-long, 20-foot-high sculpture, in Moderns Museet, Stockholm. You can walk around in her.
The Balinese say: "We have no art. We do everything as well as we can." Museum curator: "I wouldn't be seen dead with a living work of art." A. K. Coomaraswamy: "We are proud of our museums where we display a way of living that we have made impossible."
The Establishment pays homage to four anti-environmental lads. British Prime Minister Wilson visits the Cavern Club in Liverpool where the Beatles got their start. The museum has become a storehouse of human values, a cultural bloodbank.
